title: White matter pathways and social cognition.

assets.abstract: There is a growing consensus that social cognition and behavior emerge from interactions across distributed regions of the "social brain". Researchers have traditionally focused their attention on functional response properties of these gray matter networks and neglected the vital role of white matter connections in establishing such networks and their functions. In this article, we conduct a comprehensive review of prior research on structural connectivity in social neuroscience and highlight t

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
